mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X7 xDrive40d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X7 xDrive40d engine (B57D30B) typically costs £3,000–£4,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. A brand‑new BMW X7 engine sourced through an authorised dealer can represent one of the largest single repair bills a BMW owner will ever face. Replacement costs typically range from £2,500 to more than £10,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X7 xDrive40d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The xDrive40d (340 hp 3.0‑litre diesel with 48V mild‑hybrid) from MY21 onwards is the reliability champion in the X7 range. It returns 33 to 38 mpg on the motorway, has the cleanest DPF history of the diesel options, and the integrated starter‑generator avoids the low‑speed issues older diesels suffer. However, like all modern diesels, it has potential issues – EGR cooler failure (subject to recalls), AdBlue system problems, DPF blockage from urban driving, and oil leaks.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X7 xDrive40d?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are EGR cooler failure (subject to recalls – a cracked EGR cooler could create the risk of an engine fire), AdBlue system failure (pump, NOx sensors, crystallisation), DPF blockage from short urban trips, and oil leaks (valve cover gaskets, oil filter housing gaskets).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an X7 xDrive40d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the X7's retained value (typically £45,000–£70,000+), a £3,000–£5,500 reconditioned engine is a modest fraction of the vehicle's worth. Replacement is almost always financially viable. However, if the AdBlue system or EGR system has also failed, the total repair cost may be substantial. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my X7's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X7 xDrive40d uses the B57D30B (also B57D30T2 or B57D30T3) – a 3.0‑litre twin‑turbo diesel inline‑six. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The xDrive40d badge itself confirms the engine – it's the 340 PS diesel offered in the G07 X7.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair an X7 xDrive40d engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – EGR cooler failure (recall), AdBlue system problems, DPF blockage, oil leaks – are repairable. The B57 is a robust engine and major internal failures are rare. However, if the engine suffers major internal failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild. The 2021 to 2022 model years are considered the sweet spot for value and reliability.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X7 xDrive40d Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs; warranty claims; full provenance

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the X7 G07 xDrive40d generation. A brand‑new BMW X7 engine sourced through an authorised dealer can represent one of the largest single repair bills a BMW owner will ever face. Replacement costs typically range from £2,500 to more than £10,000 depending on the engine model and installation requirements.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X7 xDrive40d engine typically takes 12‑16 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). The X7's large SUV layout adds complexity compared to saloon models.

cta.label: → Compare X7 xDrive40d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (B57 — Subject to Recall)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss without visible external leaks, white smoke from exhaust, reduced engine power, engine warning light, potential fire risk.
problems.repairCost: £0 (if recalled — dealer repair free) / £500–£1,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (if out of warranty).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored coolant loss can cause engine damage).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you notice coolant loss, white smoke, or warning lights, book an inspection immediately. A cracked EGR cooler could create the risk of an engine fire.

problems.recommendation: Due to a manufacturing defect, the Exhaust Gas Recirculation (EGR) cooler may develop an internal coolant leak. If this occurs, the leaking coolant and recirculated exhaust gas may combine, which may result in a reduction of engine power and potentially a fire while driving. BMW has issued recalls for EGR cooler issues affecting multiple diesel models including the X7 xDrive40d. Check your VIN against the recall database immediately. If you notice any coolant loss, white smoke, or warning lights, have the vehicle inspected promptly.

problems.title: AdBlue System Failure (B57)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, AdBlue warnings, reduced power, the car failing to start (AdBlue tank showing empty despite being filled), NOx sensor failure.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (AdBlue pump, sensor, or dosing unit replacement).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if AdBlue warnings appear. If the AdBlue tank shows empty and the car won't start, this is a critical failure.

problems.recommendation: The B57 engine uses an AdBlue (SCR) system to reduce NOx emissions. Common issues include AdBlue pump failure, NOx sensor failure, and crystallisation blocking the dosing unit. Owners have reported cases where the AdBlue tank appeared empty despite being filled, preventing the engine from starting. Regular servicing and using high‑quality AdBlue can help prevent issues. If the engine warning light appears with AdBlue‑related codes or the car won't start, have the system diagnosed promptly.

problems.title: DPF Blockage (Urban Driving)
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light, DPF warning message, black smoke on acceleration, failed emissions test.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if DPF warning appears.

problems.recommendation: The B57 diesel is sensitive to driving patterns. If used primarily for short urban trips, the Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) will clog due to lack of proper regeneration cycles. Common symptoms include reduced power, excessive smoke, and warning lights. A defective exhaust back pressure sensor can also prevent DPF regeneration. Regular motorway runs (30+ minutes at 60+ mph) are essential to maintain the exhaust filtration system. If a DPF warning appears, a forced regeneration or cleaning may be required.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing (B57)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300–£900 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: Valve cover gaskets and oil filter housing gaskets are known leak points on both petrol and diesel X7 models. Because the X7 engine sits deep within the bay, small leaks can be hard to spot until smells or smoke appear. If the oil filter housing gasket fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break — which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. A £300–£900 gasket replacement is far cheaper than the consequences of a snapped belt. Regular oil level checks are essential.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X7 xDrive40d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Recall repair is free; out‑of‑warranty repair still cheaper than engine replacement
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Rare on B57, but if it occurs, catch it early
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW X7 xDrive40d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* EGR recall work verified as completed — this is critical
* Evidence of regular motorway driving (DPF‑friendly usage)
* AdBlue system serviced and NOx sensors checked
* 2021–2022 model year — the sweet spot for value and reliability
avoidIf: * No evidence of recall completion — EGR cooler failure is a safety issue
* Any sign of AdBlue warnings — could indicate expensive system failure
* DPF warnings on the dashboard — indicates urban‑only driving
* Poor service history — the B57 is intolerant of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X7 xDrive40d represents the sweet spot in the X7 range — the most reliable engine option with 340 PS and 700 Nm of effortless torque. The B57 is BMW's most refined diesel, and the 2021–2022 model years with 48V mild‑hybrid technology are considered the sweet spot for value and reliability. However, modern diesel complexities bring potential issues — EGR cooler failure (subject to recalls), AdBlue system problems, DPF blockage from urban driving, and oil leaks. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented recall completion, regular oil changes, and motorway usage is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare X7 xDrive40d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X7 xDrive40d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any unusual noise? (B57 is generally quiet — any rattle is a concern)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Recall Status — EGR cooler recall completed? This is safety‑critical
4. AdBlue System — Any AdBlue warnings? Has the system been serviced?
5. DPF Health — Has the vehicle done regular longer runs? (short trips kill DPFs)
6. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
7. Coolant Level — Any sign of coolant loss? (EGR cooler warning)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
